task_completion.description: Verify that Stellarium is configured for the correct operational area and time.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium location window or bottom status bar shows coordinates at or near 34.00° N, 18.00° E, and the date/time is set to 2024-11-15 03:00 (UTC).

task_completion.description: Verify that the ground/landscape display is disabled to show an unobstructed sea horizon.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The ground texture is removed in Stellarium, allowing stars to be visible below the standard horizon line.

task_completion.description: Verify that the azimuthal grid and constellation lines are enabled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A circular grid (azimuthal) radiating from the zenith and lines connecting stars into constellations are clearly visible on the Stellarium sky map.

task_completion.description: Verify that screenshots of the three target stars were captured and saved to the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal, or image viewer shows image files saved in ~/Pictures/stellarium/. The screenshots should display the stars Sirius, Polaris, and Capella (typically identifiable by Stellarium's selection markers or labels).

task_completion.description: Verify that the briefing document was generated with the required information.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output shows the contents of ~/Desktop/periscope_fix_plan.txt. The text must include 'Mediterranean Sea', the planned fix date/time (Nov 15, 2024, 03:00 UTC), and the names of the three target stars (Sirius, Polaris, Capella).

task_completion.description: Verify that the Stellarium application was closed upon completion.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the final screenshots of the trajectory, the Stellarium window is closed and its icon is no longer active in the system taskbar/dock.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually interacted with the Stellarium GUI to configure settings and view stars.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Stellarium interface open with menus, settings windows, or search dialogs being actively used, rather than just terminal commands attempting to spoof configuration files.

integrity.description: Ensure the star screenshots are genuinely captured from the configured Stellarium instance.
integrity.visual_evidence: The saved screenshots match the visual style of Stellarium with the required grid and constellation lines visible, rather than being generic images downloaded from the web.

integrity.description: Ensure the briefing document was created by the agent.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a text editor (like gedit, nano, vim) or terminal echo/cat commands being used to write the specific required text into periscope_fix_plan.txt.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Location: 34.00° N, 18.00° E. Target Date/Time: 2024-11-15 03:00:00 UTC. Required Display Settings: Landscape/ground disabled (flag_landscape: false), azimuthal grid enabled (flag_azimuthal_grid: true), constellation drawing enabled (flag_constellation_drawing: true). Target Stars: Sirius, Polaris, Capella. Output Paths: Screenshots must be in /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium, Briefing document must be at /home/ga/Desktop/periscope_fix_plan.txt.
